title: Silky Salty Italian Spaghetti Pasta
description: A quick, paleo-friendly Italian pasta that blends the buttery smoothness of peanut butter with the aromatic heat of tandoori spices for a silky, salty bite.
instructions: Cook the chickpea spaghetti in boiling water for 8–10 minutes or until al dente, then drain and set aside.
Meanwhile toast the pine nuts in a dry skillet over medium heat until golden, about 2 minutes; transfer to a plate to cool.
Heat 1 tablespoon of peanut oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat. Add minced garlic and grated ginger, sautéing for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in the tandoori spice blend and diced sun‑dried tomatoes; cook for 1 minute to release flavors.
Add the remaining tablespoon of peanut oil, peanut butter, coconut aminos, lemon juice, and sea salt. Stir until the peanut butter melts and a silky sauce forms, heating the mixture to 80–85 °C.
Toss the cooked spaghetti into the skillet, coating it thoroughly with the sauce. Fold in the toasted pine nuts and chopped basil; stir until the pasta is heated through.
Remove from heat, taste, and adjust salt if needed. Let the dish rest for 2 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Serve immediately, garnishing with extra basil if desired.
